input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install the .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (latest .NET 8 LTS or .NET 9)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8
# or: winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# See what workloads are available / installed
dotnet workload search
dotnet workload list

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target dependency)

Windows-target MAUI apps require the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling. The easiest path:

```powershell
# Install VS 2022 Build Tools with required components
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add the MAUI and UWP/WinUI workloads via the installer:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktopBuildTools `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.MSIX.Packaging `
  --passive --norestart
```

> **Or**, if you use full Visual Studio, select the **.NET Multi-platform App UI development** workload in the installer — it bundles everything.

## 4. Install the Android SDK & Emulator

MAUI's workload installs Android SDK pieces under `$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk`, but you can also manage it explicitly:

```powershell
# Discover where the Android SDK landed
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
$sdkManager = "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# List available packages (discover versions dynamically)
& $sdkManager --list

# Install the pieces you need (adjust API level as needed)
& $sdkManager "platform-tools" `
              "platforms;android-34" `
              "build-tools;34.0.0" `
              "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" `
              "emulator"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkManager --licenses    # answer 'y' to each

# Set ANDROID_HOME so tools can find it
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
$env:ANDROID_HOME = $androidSdk
```

## 5. Create & Start an Android Emulator

```powershell
$avdManager = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"

# List available device definitions
& $avdManager list device

# Create an AVD
& $avdManager create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" `
  -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" `
  -d "pixel_7"

# Launch the emulator (runs in background)
Start-Process "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" `
  -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_MAUI -gpu host"
```

## 6. Enable Windows Developer Mode (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading MSIX during development
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

> Run this in an **elevated** (Admin) PowerShell.

## 7. Create, Build & Run a MAUI App

```powershell
# Scaffold a new project
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Deploy to running emulator / connected device
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Install -p:AdbTarget="-e"
```

> Replace `net8.0-*` with `net9.0-*` if you installed .NET 9.

## 8. Discover the Right TFM Dynamically

If you're unsure which target framework monikers your SDK supports:

```powershell
# See what your project targets
grep -i TargetFrameworks *.csproj

# List installed Android/Windows SDK versions
dotnet --list-sdks
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>$null |
  Select-String "platforms;android-"

# List installed Windows SDK versions
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" |
  Select-Object Name
```

## Quick Checklist

| Component | Verify with |
|---|---|
| .NET SDK | `dotnet --version` |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload list` |
| Android SDK | `& $sdkManager --list` |
| Emulator | `& $avdManager list avd` |
| Dev Mode | Settings → Privacy & Security → Developer Mode |
| Windows App SDK | `dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0` succeeds |

Run through steps 1–6 in order, then step 7 to validate everything works end-to-end.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a thorough, practical guide.
